strengths: Maintained consistent traction with Maryland grasper throughout the cutting process
Demonstrated good scissor control with steady cutting motions
Successfully followed the circular pattern for most of the circumference
weaknesses: Failed to complete the circle within the 300-second time limit
Left multiple connection points that prevented full circle removal
Appeared to lose momentum in the final cutting phases

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, focusing on complete separation of each segment.
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to complete the circle within time limit.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle for more efficient cutting.
drills.targets_weakness: Left multiple connection points preventing removal.

drills.name: Edge-entry rehearsal
drills.description: 15 starts from the gauze edge to a marked line without overshoot, emphasizing clean entry and exit points.
drills.targets_weakness: Lost momentum in final cutting phases.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your good cutting technique into complete task execution within time limits. Practice breaking the circle into manageable segments while maintaining cutting efficiency throughout.
